raw_output:
Looking at the target agreement (contract_33), I need to analyze each provision carefully.

<document_id>contract_33</document_id>

- **q01**: The MAE definition includes a disproportionate impact proviso for clauses (i)–(vi): "except to the extent such effects directly or indirectly resulting from, arising out of, attributable to or related to the matters described in the foregoing clauses (i) through (vi) disproportionately adversely affect such Party."
- **q02**: Section 6.3(e)(iii) requires the competing proposal "did not otherwise arise from a breach of the obligations set forth in this Section 6.3" — this is any breach of no-shop.
- **q03**: The ordinary course definition mentions "consistent with past practice" only in a parenthetical about COVID exclusions, not as an actual qualifier.
- **q04**: Section 7.2(a)(iii) requires R&Ws true and correct "as of the date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date."
- **q05**: Clauses (iv) (natural disasters) and (v) (war/terrorism) are within (i)–(vi) subject to disproportionate impact.
- **q06**: Section 8.3(d)(i)(A) requires "publicly announced or publicly disclosed" for the No-Vote trigger; the breach trigger (i)(B) uses broader "announced, disclosed or otherwise communicated" language.
- **q07**: Natural disaster (clause iv) and war/terrorism (clause v) are carved out; no explicit "act of God" or "force majeure."
- **q08**: Clause (vi) (changes in law) is within (i)–(vi) subject to disproportionate impact.
- **q09**: "knowledge" means "the actual knowledge of" listed individuals.
- **q10**: Section 7.2(a)(ii) has materiality scrape for capitalization R&Ws; (iii) has scrape for general R&Ws.
- **q11**: No provision deeming disclosure schedule matters as carveout from MAE.
- **q12**: Clauses (i) and (ii) (general economic/financial conditions) are within (i)–(vi) subject to disproportionate impact.
- **q13**: Section 7.2(a)(iii) requires R&Ws true and correct except where inaccuracies would not "individually or in the aggregate, reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect" — MAE standard with scrape.
- **q14**: Clause (viii) covers "announcement," "pendency," and "consummation."
- **q15**: Section 6.1(a) uses "commercially reasonable efforts."
- **q16**: Clause (iv) includes "(including the existence, impact or worsening of COVID-19)."
